## Summary
The CMB Cold Spot is a statistically anomalous region ~10° across, significantly colder than ΛCDM predictions. Its origin is debated between supervoid ISW and primordial explanations.

## Evidence
Confirmed by Planck with p-value ~0.01. A supervoid at z ≈ 0.15 is aligned but accounts for only 10–20% of the temperature decrement.

## Significance
Could indicate non-Gaussian initial conditions, topological defects, or bubble collision signatures from eternal inflation.
